title: build_request/extract_response call-id coupling is implicit
description: build_request() hardcodes call-id 'r1'. extract_response() is then called with literal 'r1' at every callsite in methods.rs. The comment says not to add multi-call batching without updating extract_response, but the invariant is not enforced by the type system. A contributor adding a new method will copy the pattern and may accidentally pass the wrong call-id string to extract_response — extracting the wrong invocation. If the argument shapes are compatible, no compile error and no test failure unless the test checks the exact field values. Maintenance consequence: silent wrong-invocation bugs in new method wrappers that pass code review because the pattern looks correct.
